audit_items.proof_trace: All free operations in multi.c are sound: freeClientMultiState is always followed by initClientMultiState (via discardTransaction) which nulls c->mstate.commands, or is called from freeClient/freeFakeClient where the client struct is immediately discarded — in both cases no dangling pointer is reachable. The unwatchAllKeys iterator uses the documented safe listDelNode-during-iteration pattern (adlist.c:listNext pre-fetches iter->next before returning the current node, and the list's free callback is NULL so zfree(wk) on the value remains valid). execCommand restores c->argv = orig_argv (L185) before calling discardTransaction (L188), so c->argv never points into freed mstate on any code path including the handle_monitor label.
